name: Berkeley County Public Schools
description_short: Berkeley County Public Schools Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, non-profit financial cooperative serving Berkeley County employees and their families since 1973.
description_long:
Berkeley County Public Schools Federal Credit Union (BCPSFCU) was established in 1973 as a member-owned, non-profit financial cooperative. The institution is governed by a member-elected Board of Directors that meets monthly to set policy, establish interest rates, and declare dividends. All deposits are federally insured to at least $250,000 by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA), backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government.

BCPSFCU offers a comprehensive range of financial products and services including share savings accounts, share draft checking accounts, vehicle loans (new and used), signature loans, VISA debit cards, Christmas Club and Vacation Club savings accounts, and optional credit disability and credit life insurance on loans. Members enjoy free bill pay, free e-statements, free notary services, and flexible payroll deduction options. Online and mobile banking platforms provide convenient account access.

The credit union distinguishes itself through its inclusive membership philosophy: "once a member, always a member." Immediate family members of primary members—including spouses, children, parents, siblings, grandparents, and grandchildren—are eligible to join. Membership requires a minimal $5.00 initial deposit to a shares savings account and a one-time $5.00 non-refundable membership fee. The organization maintains a local presence with one location in Berkeley County and operates during standard business hours, Monday–Friday with extended hours on Wednesday.

BCPSFCU is best suited for Berkeley County Public Schools employees and their families seeking straightforward, member-focused financial services with low fees and NCUA protection. The institution's small scale and local governance mean fewer products and features compared to larger regional or national credit unions, and members should be aware that the credit union maintains limited branch access and modest online functionality.
services: Share Savings Accounts (membership establishment, $5.00 minimum deposit)
Share Draft Accounts (checking, no minimum balance, no per-check fees, no monthly fees)
VISA Debit Cards with courtesy protection
Christmas Club savings (annual maturity approximately October 15)
Vacation Club/Summer Savings (annual maturity approximately June 25)
Direct Deposit and Payroll Deduction options
Credit Disability Insurance and Credit Life Insurance on loans
pros: NCUA-insured deposits up to $250,000 backed by federal government
No minimum balance required for share draft checking accounts
No per-check fees or monthly service charges on checking
Free bill pay and free e-statements included
Low membership barrier—only $5.00 minimum deposit to open
Extended family eligibility: spouses, children, parents, siblings, grandparents, grandchildren can join
Free notary services available to members
Lifetime membership with no forced closure if account remains active
cons: Single physical location in Berkeley County limits accessibility
Limited online and mobile banking functionality compared to larger institutions
Restricted membership: primarily for Berkeley County Public Schools employees and their families
Basic product suite—no investment accounts, wealth management, or business banking services listed
Closed daily 1:00 PM–2:00 PM for lunch, reducing accessibility during business hours
best_for: Berkeley County Public Schools employees seeking safe, federally-insured savings and basic lending
Families of school district employees wanting low-cost checking and vehicle financing
Individuals prioritizing local, member-owned financial institutions over large corporate banks
Members seeking straightforward products without complex fees or minimum balance requirements
diagnosis: BCPSFCU is ideal for Berkeley County Public Schools employees and their immediate families seeking secure, low-cost basic banking and lending services with federal insurance protection. The main caveat is membership exclusivity and limited geographic accessibility—the credit union operates only one physical location with restricted hours and basic digital platforms, making it less suitable for individuals seeking comprehensive financial services or those outside the eligible employee/family network.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Berkeley County Public Schools (Martinsburg, WV) | CreditDoc
meta_description: Berkeley County Public Schools — NCUA-insured credit union in Martinsburg, WV. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
